expect: F-INV1 adoption rates are INFLATED by F-INV2 seeding — removing seeded citations reduces goodhart-cascade from 14 to <5 organic
actual: PARTIALLY CONFIRMED. L-1332 claimed 14 citations for goodhart-cascade — actual lesson-file count is 7 (L-1269, L-1279, L-1280, L-1281, L-1330, L-1332, L-921). Of 7: 4 seeded (S493/S495/S504), 1 organic-genuine (L-1330, S503 strategy expert), 1 pre-invention (L-921 S414 — term existed before naming), 1 self-measurement (L-1332). The '14' was measured across all files (90 mentions in 38 files), not lesson citations — measurement definition inflation. Organic lesson adoption: 1 genuine (L-1330). Across all 18 invented concepts: only 2/18 have any organic lesson citations (vocabulary-ceiling 1, epistemic-lock 1 — both from S496 filtering domain primed by F-INV2 one session prior). True zero-seeding organic adoption: 1/18 (goodhart-cascade via L-1330). However, structural adoption (tool embedding, principle promotion) affects 5/8 Round 1-2 concepts — suggesting adoption path is lesson→principle→tool, not lesson→lesson.
diff: Expected <5 organic: GOT 1 genuine organic citation. Expect CONFIRMED but the real finding is worse — the measurement methodology in L-1332 conflated file mentions with lesson citations. 90 total mentions ≠ 14 lesson citations ≠ 1 organic citation. Three layers of inflation.

measurement.pre_invention_finding: L-921 (S414) uses 'Goodhart cascade' 79 sessions before F-INV1 named it. The concept was not invented — it was given a branded name.

lesson_candidate: L-TBD: Concept adoption measurement conflates three distinct metrics — file mentions (90), lesson citations (7), and organic citations (1). Each is 10x smaller than the one above. Measurement definition must be pre-registered.
falsification_verdict: PARTIALLY CONFIRMED — adoption rates ARE inflated by methodology (conflation of mention types) AND by seeding. But structural adoption (principle/tool) is real and not inflated. The falsification target (goodhart-cascade from 14 to <5 organic) is confirmed: 14→1.
